channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Undead Chronicles Will Be “Miserable, Creepy, and Depressive”, and That’s Great
item.content:encoded:
Undead Chronicles is a ‘slow-burn horror FPS’ that’s gathering traction on Steam. It’s approaching 100,000 wishlist spots, has a demo landing this October, and is shaping up nicely in preview materials. It has been on my radar for a hot minute on account of the way it’s approaching the concept of a zombie apocalypse.

It’s not a run-and-gun action title, nor is it a run-of-the-mill survival shooter. It’s a brutal, realistic, and weighted end-of-the-world FPS that encourages you to shoot straight, watch your back, and accept that there is absolutely no mercy to be found from the undead.

I recently caught up with the game’s creator, Seyfettin Dincturk, to learn more about Undead Chronicles.

The Inspiration Behind Undead Chronicles

In a post on social media, Seyfettin Dincturk, the creator of Undead Chronicles, explained that the game is going to be ‘miserable, creepy, and depressive’. It’s going for a darker vibe than most zombie shooters accommodate these days, feeding more into the ‘Romero’ shamblers that popularized the concept of the undead several decades ago.

Undead Chronicles started life four years ago, taking shape as a pixel art game, but Dincturk pivoted from Unity to Unreal Engine 5, rebuilding the game as a quality first-person shooter.

Today, Undead Chronicles is being pieced together by a very small team that’s ‘passionate about single-player games.’ It has grown organically, and it’s fast becoming one of the most-wanted horror shooters coming to Steam.

I asked Dincturk what inspired Undead Chronicles:

We really love the vibe of (The Last of Us), Bloodborne, Resident Evil, and ZombiU…

Most zombie games involve too much comedy to compensate for very long completion hours and to also compensate for the price tag.

I believe there is a market for a depressive story, where you are all by yourself, stuck in 1923, shooting the undead.

It’s a simple, back-to-basics premise, and that’s one of the reasons I love it. It’s just cold, hard horror, planting players in a dire situation within an eerie world and demanding that they survive with what they have to hand.

The game’s description panel on Steam helps paint a picture of the world in Undead Chronicles:

They do not explode or mutate. They behave like broken people. Some panic. Some charge. The shotgun exists for the moments when distance disappears.

Restore power by hunting fusebox pieces. Find keys hidden in jacket pockets, behind paintings, and inside desk drawers. Every drawer opens, unless it’s locked. No crowbars. No ritual candles. Just logic and observation.

There’s obviously something of a jab there at games like Resident Evil, which have replicated the same basic puzzle designs for many years. That’s not necessarily a bad thing, but it’s not the operating model that Seyfettin’s team is going for.

  • RELATED: Capcom Not Interested in Resi 5 & 6 Remakes

Undead Chronicles takes real places rooted in history and turns them upside down amid the apocalypse. The game innovatively uses shadow and darkness, with a lack of light preventing your progress, forcing you to adapt as you move. At first blush, it looks like a solid horror FPS with plenty of potential.

Do you think Undead Chronicles looks like your kind of game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: First Major Windrose Content Update Detailed in New Post
item.content:encoded:
The team at Kraken Express has revealed everything you could want to know regarding the first major content update for Windrose, the open-world pirate-themed survival game that stormed up the charts just a few months ago. The game was released into early access on April 14, and it has carved out a nice space for itself in the survival niche thus far.

In the post on Steam, the team at Kraken uncovered everything coming in the Ashlands update, including the expansion of the story, new ships, and improvements to the game’s core operating model.

Windrose ‘Ashlands’ Update Explained by Devs

To open the blog post on Steam, the team at Kraken Express explained that Windrose’s story will be getting a sizeable buff when the Ashlands update drops. New factions will be introduced, and both the main story and side quests will be expanded nicely. They’re taking on the task of amending the existing factions too, making them more detailed and unique.

With the Ashlands update comes the Ashlands biome, a new region in the game, pitted and scarred by volcanic eruptions. It’s a dangerous place to explore, but it’s bursting with high-tier loot and plenty of resources for those who dare tread there.

Kraken is exploring new ways to expand the concept of base-building in Windrose, stressing that they’ve been inspired by what the community has managed to create thus far. They want to foster that creativity and let it bloom, so watch this space.

The team then admitted a weak spot that’s being addressed:

We feel that our ship gameplay has a lot of room for expansion, and we also have tons of your ideas and requests to draw from. It won’t be possible to tuck everything we’d like into one update, but we’ll surely experiment with and implement a few additions to this part of Windrose.

With that said, they’re trying to enrich and deepen ship gameplay, add more ship classes, and give combat gameplay ‘more care and attention’. There are also improvements inbound for NPC ships, which should reflect in more natural behaviour.

Are you still playing Windrose? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: All Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Changes Compared to Original
item.content:encoded:
This is the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag you all remember. However, in classic remake fashion, Ubisoft has decided to make some necessary quality-of-life improvements for the betterment of you, the player. I’ve tracked down all the changes I can find over the OG.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will resemble the 2013 game, but modernize it with flashier graphics, smoother performance (hopefully), and tweak certain aspects of the original that may not have aged well.

Biggest Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Differences

The biggest changes in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced include the Animus, movement, traversal, combat, and mission design alterations, along with new gameplay and many more customization options.

90-95% of Black Flag Resynced is the core game, just reshaped here and there. But it doesn’t hurt for Ubisoft to tinker with 15-year-old gameplay to make it more palatable in 2026.

Here’s a deeper look at the changes, outside of the obvious graphical overhaul.

Animus Altered

The Animus has undergone some maintenance, which will, ‘Feature new moments that focus on Edward’s internal struggle.’

Recent AC games have shifted away from the Animus, as players feel it takes them away from the game they’re enjoying. So, it’s still in Resynced, but has been reworked, with new bits added in.

Action-Based Combat

Resyned’s combat has had an overhaul, with the ability to use the Hidden Blade during combat being removed and now restricted to stealth kills and specific prompts where it’s required.

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Editions, Prices & Pre-Order Bonuses

Generally, Black Flag Resynced’s combat is ‘Reworked To An Action-Oriented Experience.’

This isn’t as scary as it sounds. Combos should be smooth, and there’s a modern-day action focus on parrying and colours to signify everything. It’ll be second nature very quickly.

Furthermore, enemies adapt to repetitive playstyles.

Mission Design Tweaks

One aspect, in particular, has received a much-needed revision in Resynced: Revamped Tailing and Eavesdropping Missions.

Here’s what Edward Kenway voice actor Matt Ryan had to say:

“Tailing and eavesdropping missions have also been revamped. In the original game, getting discovered meant instant desynchronization. Yes, this was a pretty big pinpoint we remember. Now, the action continues, your objctives remain, but your target will react, and you must adapt accordingly.”

Movement

There’s a more fluid feel about Edward in Resynced, which we’ve seen from leaked gameplay footage.

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Day 1 Update & Patch Notes

It’s been confirmed that you can now crouch, aiding stealth, and your movement will be affected by the game’s dynamic day and night cycle, with darkness giving you an edge—a two-for-one here.

Naval Upgrades & Sea Exploration

You can recruit officers for the Jackdaw, which is cool! More ships, more customization options to make your ship look boss, new sea shanties, and so much more.

Resynced as a whole has been described by the devs as ‘completely seamless,’ and it should mean next-to-no loading screens and full freedom to explore the game.

Hours of New Gameplay

If you didn’t know, the new Black Flag Resynced content adds six hours of gameplay.

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Length & Total Hours

Richard Knight, game director for AC Resynced, has confirmed that new content includes new quests, officer quests, and other similar activities—we’re sure a lot of it is being kept as a surprise for the full release.

New Trophies

Another small change is that the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Trophy List is different compared to the original. Many are the same, but a few new ones have been added as you chase the Platinum Trophy or full Gamerscore.

Which change has you the most intrigued about Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced? Let us know via the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming Resynced content, check out our Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Preload and File Sizes guide, and be sure to sign up for our free Weekly Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Backyard Baseball Will Feature Trading Cards Designed By Independent Artists—Insider Access
item.content:encoded:
Kids around the world remember what it was like to earn some allowance money, take it down to the local card shop, and buy a new pack of cards to add to their collection. You never knew who you were going to get, or what the cards would look like. It was a joyous feeling every time as you hoped to pull a rare rookie card or even a jersey patch.

Backyard Baseball wants to bring that feeling back.

As part of our Insider Access series that gives you an exclusive first look at a new game or feature, Insider Gaming can exclusively reveal the token reward system for trading cards coming to Backyard Baseball when it releases on PC via Steam on July 9.

Baseball Cards With Hand-Drawn Artwork

For weeks, the Backyard Sports team has been teasing this feature on social media. In various posts featuring the clubhouse, there’s been a jar labeled “TOKENS 4 CARDS”. As you play, whether it be Backyard Derby, Season Mode, or Quick Play, you’ll earn various amounts of tokens. These tokens can then be exchanged for packs of baseball cards.

Now, to be clear, these aren’t microtransaction purchases. Backyard Baseball is a game with zero microtransactions. Instead, everything you unlock and purchase in the game is earned through gameplay and nothing else.

When you get enough Tokens, you’ll be able to buy a pack that features one of four types of cards across three categories—Aces, Speedsteers, and Sluggers. As you buy cards, they will be added to your card binder, each having unique writeups and voice lines.

One of the types of cards, dubbed “REMIX”, is an artist series that reimagines your favorite backyard sports characters in a new style. That style comes from independent artists that Playground Productions has spent months collaborating with. Each design is hand-drawn and unique, and every player has one.

  • More Backyard Baseball: Backyard Baseball’s Comeback Isn’t Just About Nostalgia

“Backyard Baseball has always celebrated the fun of the sport, and that includes collecting baseball cards,” Playground Productions CEO Lindsay Barnett told Insider Gaming. “The new card-collecting reward system highlights the hand-drawn artwork that has defined the franchise for decades, while encouraging players to explore the game and build their collections along the way.”

Backyard Baseball launches on July 9 for PC. A console release is coming soon after, but a firm date hasn’t been announced.

In other news, read about how Asetek Racing is taking aim at sim racing’s closed ecosystems. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Win a Championship Gaming Chair with Insider Gaming Esports
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming Esports is excited to partner with AutoFull to give one lucky reader the chance to win the chair that hosted the champions during the recent IEM Cologne tournament. On the stage in Germany, teams sat atop the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0, an ergonomic and advanced esports gaming chair with incredible features.

Below, you’ll learn how you can enter the giveaway and potentially win a stunning gaming chair that, out of sale, retails for a whopping $899.99. In our recent review, we wrote, ‘you’ll not find many chairs that offer the standard that the M6 Ultra 2.0 does.’

How to Enter The Giveaway for the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0 Chair

If you want to be in with a chance of winning the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0 chair with Insider Gaming Esports, you need to complete two simple steps.

First, you’ll need to follow Insider Gaming Esports on X.

Then, you’ll need to join the Insider Gaming Discord server and head to the ‘Giveaways’ channel to officially log your participation.

The entry window runs from July 1st to Monday, July 6th. When that window has closed, a winner will be automatically picked from the entrants on Discord, and the winner will be immediately notified. At that point, our friends at AutoFull will facilitate shipping a chair directly to the winner.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server what you think about the AutoFull M6 Ultra 2.0 chair, and make sure you complete both steps above to have your entry counted for this giveaway!

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the full Esports World Cup 2026 schedule, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming newsletter for the latest news available

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: CINDER CITY is The Division Meets Stellar Blade, With a PvE Twist
item.content:encoded:
CINDER CITY has just landed on Steam, at the same time as the developers at BigFire Games shared an in-depth preview of this upcoming open-world shooter. As one of these exciting games coming in under the radar, CINDER CITY might not have landed on your wishlist just yet, but it certainly has managed to find a spot on mine!

BigFire Games is a subsidiary of NC, the game’s publisher, and CINDER CITY is the team’s first game. It looks like an ambitious starting effort, taking elements from games like The Division and Stellar Blade and fusing them in a gritty, post-apocalyptic world.

Become a CINDERKNIGHT in CINDER CITY

CINDER CITY is set to burst into the MMORPG space at some point, bringing years of experience from NC’s teams and using them to flesh out a post-apocalyptic world in ‘near-future’ Seoul.

The Steam page that went live for CINDER CITY on June 30 paints the best picture possible of this game.

It’s a tactical third-person shooter that pits players against monstrous creatures in the fight to take back Seoul, which has been overrun. As players fight, either alone or with others, they’ll make use of vehicles, mech suits, and even aircraft ‘scattered across the open world.’

With a choice and consequence model at the heart of the game, players will pick their missions, take on dynamic events, and explore CINDER CITY how they see fit. And, as the game’s brief promises, ‘the choices you make will shape what this city becomes.’

  • RELATED: Learn How The Division Started Life

You’ve got all the trappings of a typical MMO in CINDER CITY, including dungeons, world raids, immense bosses, and a role-based system that leans into the traditional structure of the genre.

These elements sit nicely alongside gameplay that’s reminiscent of both The Division and Stellar Blade. The latter’s influence can be felt in some of the game’s hulking creatures, and all three games have a similar post-apocalyptic streak running through them.

Here’s the most recent preview for CINDER CITY:

Does CINDER CITY look like your kind of game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: When Will PS3 & PS Vita Online Stores Close? All Regions & Dates
item.content:encoded:
It’s a sad day for PlayStation fans, as it’s been revealed that the digital storefronts for the PlayStation 3 and PlayStation Vita are shutting down. Up until now, they’ve been online, with business running as usual for the older generation of consoles and handhelds, but the tough decision to turn them offline has been made.

As mentioned in the official statement, Sony notes that the call has been made due to updated payment processing standards. Unfortunately, the PS3 and PS Vita aren’t able to support these updates at the level required, leading to the closure of their associated PlayStation Stores.

That said, they’re not shutting down immediately, so you still have time to purchase and download digital titles before the stores go offline for good. With that in mind, here’s when the PS3 and PS Vita online stores close, across all regions.

When Will The PlayStation 3 Store Close?

The PlayStation 3 digital store will begin to close in August 2026 for select regions, though for most of the world, you’ll have until 2027 to make final purchases.

Specific dates haven’t been provided just yet, but PlayStation has given us months as windows for the closures. Latin America and the Middle East suffer first, with the digital store closing this year, whilst the rest of the world has until 2027.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Will PlayStation Stop Making Discs? PlayStation Store Changes, Explained

For the full PS3 store closure timeline, check out the table below:

When Will The PS Vita Store Close?

It’s the same story for the PlayStation Vita; the digital store will begin to close in August 2026, with final closures scheduled for July 2027.

There is a great selection of handheld games available and exclusive to the PS Vita. If you still have one collecting dust at home, or never got your hands on the handheld, now might be the perfect opportunity, as you’re on the clock to make some final digital purchases from its PlayStation Store.

  • More Insider Gaming: PlayStation’s No Disc Plans for 2028 Could Hint at PS6 Release Date

For the full PS Vita store closure timeline, check out the table below:

Losing a digital store is always a sad moment for gamers, but everything has to come to an end eventually. Still, what an era that generation of console was…

Will you be making any final purchases for the PS3 or PS Vita? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Review Embargo Date, and be sure to sign up for our free Weekly Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/when-will-ps3-ps-vita-online-stores-close-all-regions-dates/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: When Will PlayStation Stop Making Discs? PlayStation Store Changes, Explained
item.content:encoded:
In a recent update, the team at Sony Interactive Entertainment revealed a drastic shift coming for the world of PlayStation. In a paradigm change, PlayStation is moving away from physical discs to an all-digital landscape, ceasing the production of discs in response to ‘shifting trends in consumer preference.’

This is a massive overhaul to the gaming space that meets the increasingly digital nature of the business head-on. It’s a fairly obvious move, but it’s sure to rub some gamers the very wrong way.

Read on to learn when PlayStation discs will stop being made and what this means for you as a gamer.

Will All Sony PlayStation Games Be Digital?

From January 2028, all new PlayStation games will be digital only, Sony confirmed in a blog post published on July 1, 2026. This means that any games released on or after January 2028 will not have a traditional disc.

Instead, publishers and retailers will have to rely on codes in a box or on the digital storefronts that have quickly become the number-one destination to buy new games.

In the blog post, the team explained:

This is a natural direction for Sony Interactive Entertainment to adapt to consumer trends as the general preference for digital media significantly outpaces physical discs. This transition will enable us to align more closely with how most of our community prefers to access and play games today.

This move does not impact any games released prior to January 2028, so don’t worry. It’s not like you’ll wake up on January 1, 2028, and be met with an inability to play discs in your PlayStation 5 console.

How Does This Change Impact Me as a Gamer?

The most critical way this impacts you as a gamer is in your purchasing habits. This will effectively eradicate most game sharing (between friends), second-hand, and physical sale markets going forward. There will still be a back catalogue, but it’ll eventually fade away over the following years.

If you’ve always loved physical media such as discs, you’ll be taking a knock when this change lands in January 2028. Every new PlayStation game you buy beyond that point will be 100% digital.

  • RELATED: No Disc Plans Hint at PS6 Release Date

It’s a safe bet that the PlayStation 6 will launch without a physical drive, but owners should have the option to bolt one on for backwards compatibility purposes, assuming the PS6 accommodates that function.

Additionally, Sony has announced the impending closure of digital stores for the PlayStation 3 and PS Vita platforms. By July 2027, both of these digital storefronts will be closed globally, bringing an end to twenty years of gaming history.

What do you think about this update? Are you mad at PlayStation for scrapping the production of discs? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that NBCUniversal might get into video games, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
